name: Rabbit (or Chicken) Jambalaya
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut meat away from bones and chop into 1/4-inch pieces; use scraps, bones, and giblets (excluding the liver) to make the stock. Refrigerate meat until ready to use.
2. Combine seasoning mix ingredients in a small bowl and set aside.
3. Melt margarine in a 4-quart saucepan.
4. Add 3/4 cup of onions, celery, and bell peppers, then stir in the seasoning mix, Tabasco, and tasso.
5. Cook over high heat until onions are dark brown, about 20 minutes, stirring constantly.
6. Add the remaining 3/4 cup of onions, celery, and bell peppers. Cook about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
7. Stir in tomato sauce and simmer about 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
8. Add rabbit and cook over high heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
9. Stir in rice, mixing well. Reduce heat and simmer for about 12 minutes.
10. Add stock. Bring the mixture to a boil; reduce heat and simmer covered over very low heat until rice is tender but still firm, about 15 minutes.
11. To serve, mold rice in an 8-ounce cup. Place 2 cups on each serving plate for a main course or 1 cup for an appetizer.
12. Makes 4 main-dish or 8 appetizer servings.
